free_text: No evidence of break-up effects on the fusion of 9-Be
with medium-light nuclei.
Elastic scattering data
Statistical code PACE.
The resolution in both experiments was good
enough to separate events differing by one unity of
atomic number.
Data tables were received from J.Lubian
Data table for elastic scattering was
received from J.Lubian
Additional reference is added. FACILITY is
added to SUBENT A0554005
Last checking has been done.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
The 20UD TANDAR laboratory.
A large ionization chamber,
followed by a surface barrier detector.
The aluminum target thickness was approximately
80 ug/cm2, a thin gold layer of 10 ug/cm2 was
evaporated on it for normalization purposes.
Angular distributions were obtained at four
different angles (10, 12, 15, 20). The maxima of the
angular distributions were located near 12 degr. and
therefore the experimental angular distributions
covered around 80% of the complete angular
distribution.
The extrapolation to the most forward part of the
distributions was performed with the use of the
shape of the theoretical predictions obtained by the
statistical code PACE.
The overall fusion cross section error is
of the order of 10-15%. No information about source of
uncertainty.
With data 9Be + 29Si and 11B + 27Al
Data
presented on fig.3
SD: STATUS added. REL-REF deleted (info is
given in ADD-RES). ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
8UD Pelletron laboratory
A large anode-splitted ionization
chamber and a position sensitive silicon detector.
The 9Be target thickness was approximately 80 ug/cm2,
deposited onto carbon backing of ~10 ug/cm2, a thin
gold layer of 10 ug/cm2, was evaporated on it for
normalization purposes.
The data for 18O + 10B and 17O + 11B.
Angular distributions were obtained in the
range 7 - 37 degr. The maxima of the angular
distributions were located near 12 degr., and
therefore the experimental angular distributions
covered around 80% of the complete angular
distribution.
The extrapolation to the most forward part of the
distributions was performed with the use of the
shape of the theoretical predictions obtained by the
statistical code PACE.
The overall fusion cross section error is
of the order of 10-15%. No information about source of
uncertainty.
With data 18O + 10B and 17O + 11B.
